user_query:
Last night’s rehearsal takes didn’t get archived by my local script. I’m a rock ’n’ roll purist and I rely on this archive to keep every electric guitar and live vocal take tight. Please triage the incident and propose a reliability-safe fix using only the files in this workspace.

Inputs to inspect:
- Log: logs/archiver.log
- Script: scripts/archiver.py
- Config: config/archiver.yaml
- Incoming files directory: input/incoming/

What I need you to produce:
1) A structured incident report at output/incident_report.md with these sections and contents:
   - Summary: one-paragraph plain-English overview of what broke and why.
   - Timeline: include the timestamp of the most recent failed run from logs/archiver.log and any earlier successful run it references.
   - Impact: count how many unarchived files are currently in input/incoming/, list their exact filenames, and give counts per extension.
   - Root Cause: explain the mismatch you find between the code/config and the actual incoming files (cite the relevant config keys and how the script evaluates extensions).
   - Evidence: include at least one exact log line from logs/archiver.log that supports the failure, and quote the current allowed_extensions and case_sensitive values from config/archiver.yaml.
   - Fix & Prevention: describe the minimal config change to ensure .wav files (any case) are processed, and one preventive reliability improvement (e.g., a check or test) you’d add.

2) A fixed configuration file saved as output/archiver.fixed.yaml. Start from config/archiver.yaml but modify it so that:
   - allowed_extensions includes both ".flac" and ".wav".
   - case_sensitive is set to false so that .WAV/.Wav variants are accepted.
   Keep all other keys identical and unchanged.

3) A draft status email saved as output/status_update_email.txt for the band and the rehearsal space owner. Requirements:
   - The first line must be: "Subject: Status: Recording archiver incident 2024-04-18".
   - Body under 200 words, plain tone (no marketing), summarizing what happened, how many files were affected (use your Impact count), the fix you applied, and the next step/ETA.

Constraints:
- Do not use the Internet.
- Base all findings on the provided files and the current contents of input/incoming/.
- Do not change any files under config/, scripts/, logs/, or input/. Only write new files under output/.

Deliverables to create:
- output/incident_report.md
- output/archiver.fixed.yaml
- output/status_update_email.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
